description_long: This is a medium-sized, rounded stone with a slightly irregular, oblong shape and a predominantly light beige coloration mottled with brown and tan patches. The surface appears weathered and rough, with subtle pitting and natural textural variations typical of stones found in outdoor urban environments. One side of the stone features a visible marking or number, suggesting it may have been cataloged or used for identification purposes. The stone's edges are softened, lacking sharp corners, and its overall form is somewhat flattened along one axis, giving it a squat, block-like appearance. The coloration is uneven, with some areas showing more concentrated brownish hues, possibly from exposure to soil or environmental staining. There are no visible artificial modifications, holes, or recesses, indicating it is a solid, natural object.
description: A rounded, beige and brown mottled stone with a rough, weathered surface and a squat, oblong shape. It features natural textural variations and a visible marking on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, most rounded face of the stone, with a visible dark marking or number near the lower left edge.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the stone's oblong, block-like profile and highlights its flattened sides and mottled surface coloration.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is visible here, showing a more uniform beige surface with less prominent markings or stains.
description_view_3: This side view mirrors view 1, displaying the stone's squat, cylindrical profile and the transition between lighter and darker surface areas.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a boundary marker
attribute_pattern: Mottled pattern of beige and brown with irregular, natural speckling and a visible catalog marking on one side
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and weathered, with softened edges and some staining, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil and other stones, but the catalog marking may contrast with natural elements
